methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $58M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Foxglove, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Adrian Macneil and Roman Shtylman, has raised $58M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable, all operating in robotics and manufacturing at Series B through Series C stages. Key private peers include Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics, which provide direct stage and sector alignment.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy base, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $110M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Hadrian ($1.6B), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B)—produces a median of $1.6B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.8B.

Triangulation across both methods supports a range of $94M to $1.9B, with a median of $868M. The wide dispersion reflects material tension between capital-deployment-based valuation ($110M) and peer-comparable multiples ($1.6B median). This gap likely reflects either significant capital efficiency differences relative to peers, or valuation overshoot in the robotics sector peer set. Capital intensity, revenue traction, and path to profitability are critical refinement variables not present in this dataset.
